title: The Book of His Generations
subtitle: The genealogy of the Saviour — how God descended to us through the line of Abraham and David

stillness: Be still before the long roll of names.
One generation alone is sought among them all:
the generation by which the Word came down to you.
gospel: The book of the generation of Jesus Christ,
the son of David, the son of Abraham.
and so the line descends, even unto Joseph,
of whom was born Jesus, who is called Christ.
behold: Behold the book of His generation, and see how the names descend.
Matthew weaves the line coming down, beginning from Abraham,
for he treats of the humanity by which Christ descended to us,
and he shows how God took upon Himself the weakness that is ours.
See the manner in which God came into the world:
assuming flesh descending from the fathers,
and proceeding even to Joseph, the husband of Mary,
of whom alone, the Virgin, Jesus was born in the flesh,
for of the Father He is born in His essence.
Consider that all these generations are recounted
for the sake of that one by which Mary begot Jesus,
and that David is set before Abraham,
though in time he came after:
for David was a deep sinner,
and the sinner is set before the just,
that Christ might be shown born of mercy alone.
consider: He is called Jesus as to His divine nature,
and Christ as to His human nature.
The name of Jesus is the name of glory;
the name of Christ is the name of grace.
Through three sets of fourteen the generations proceed:
some before the Law, some under the Law,
and the last, the generation of Christ, in the time of grace.
By this it is signified that in every age men were saved,
through faith in Christ.
When thrice fourteen generations were complete,
the state of men was changed:
under judges, then under kings, then under priests —
and now from Christ the state of men is changed again,
that He Himself might be Judge, and King, and Priest.
Three sets of fourteen make forty-two generations,
by which we come to Christ, promised to us as our reward —
as the sons of Israel, through forty-two encampments,
came at last to the land promised to them.
And the first fourteen marks the soul that begins to repent;
the second, the soul going forward;
the third, the soul made perfect.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, our hope and our trust,
be mindful of what You took upon Yourself for our redemption.
Be mindful that, the Creator of all things,
You deigned to take our nature and become aware of our frailty.
You came, O Lord, for the sake of sinners,
that You might blot out the sins of all.
What shall I render to You? What can be worthily done for this?
I praise You; I give You thanks with my whole heart,
by which You came to the aid of a lost human race.
And I pray You, most merciful Lord,
that, having so bent down for us as to deign to become man,
You would not suffer to perish in us
what in mercy You resolved to assume.
Make me render to You a service grateful and owed. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quæ generationes narrantur, propter illam qua Maria genuit Jesum, qui vocatur Christus.
latinJewel.english: These generations are recounted for the sake of that one by which Mary begot Jesus, who is called Christ.
